fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: North Dakota CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to North Dakota - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity north dakota, how to transfer CNA license to north dakota, north dakota CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in North Dakota.

steps.title: Create NDBON Online Portal Account
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the NDBON Online Portal (ndbon.org/Portal) using your legal name and email address.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your NDBON Online Portal account, select the Nursing Assistant Application, and complete all required sections, including personal information and education/training details.

steps.title: Submit Required Documents & Fees
steps.description: Upload any necessary documentation (e.g., proof of training, identification) directly through the portal and pay the non-refundable application fee of $40.

steps.title: Register and Pass NNAAP Exam
steps.description: Register for the National Nurse Aide Assessment Program (NNAAP) exam through Prometric, complete both the written/oral and skills components, and achieve a passing score.

steps.title: Await Registry Listing
steps.description: Prometric will send your exam results to the NDBON. The Board will then review your application and exam results for compliance with state requirements.

steps.title: Verify Listing Status
steps.description: Monitor your application status via your NDBON Online Portal account and confirm your listing on the North Dakota Nursing Assistant Registry once approved.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does North Dakota CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $25. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in North Dakota.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to North Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Dakota while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in ND.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to North Dakota.

hero.title: Become a CNA in North Dakota
hero.dek: To become a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) in North Dakota, applicants must apply through the NDBON Online Portal, pass the National Nurse Aide Assessment Program (NNAAP) exam, and meet all state requirements for listing on the North Dakota Nursing Assistant Registry.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in North Dakota
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to North Dakota CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Applicants must register for an account using their legal name and email address on the NDBON Online Portal. After creating and verifying an account, they can log in to start the Nursing Assistant application.
